entry.title: HCM Brasil - eSocial S-1.3 - Entrega final
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Estamos orgulhosos em anunciar que concluímos a etapa final das nossas entregas relacionadas ao eSocial S-1.3.

Nesta última fase, nos dedicamos à revisão das transformações XML de todos os eventos padrão eSocial para estar de acordo com a nova versão do layout e a Nota Técnica S-1.3 Nº 01/2024. Além disso, realizamos mudanças importantes na geração e visualização dos principais eventos impactados: S-1010, S-1200, S-2299, S-2399, S-5001, S-5002, e S-5011.

Antes de darmos detalhes sobre a entrega final, gostaríamos de fazer uma rápida retrospectiva dos principais pontos abordados nos dois blog posts anteriores:

Na primeira fase focamos em entregar todas as modificações dos eventos de escopo limitado, fornecendo aos nossos clientes e parceiros mais tempo para se adaptarem aos seus sistemas. Atualizamos também o documento de entregáveis, com o detalhamento das interfaces de BAdI disponíveis para o complemento da solução.

Na segunda fase, realizamos todas as alterações relacionadas ao evento S-1210 - Pagamentos de Rendimentos do Trabalho. Isso inclui a habilidade de retificar as informações enviadas no ano anterior, sem a necessidade de reabrir os períodos anteriores.

Agora, na terceira e última fase, após a revisão das transformações XML e adaptações necessárias, finalizamos a entrega do eSocial S-1.3. Esta etapa crucial garante que nossos clientes e parceiros estejam atualizados e em conformidade com as últimas normas legais e técnicas.

Aproveitamos esta oportunidade também, para comunicar que realizamos um evento detalhando este novo layout. O evento "eSocial S-1.3 - Potencializando a experiência da solução SAP" foi uma manhã repleta de insights e conhecimentos sobre a nova versão. 

Gostaríamos de agradecer a todos nossos clientes e parceiros que se juntaram a nós neste evento. Foi extremamente valioso para todos os presentes. Você encontra a apresentação e gravação deste evento no nosso Work Zone.

Nesta etapa final, entregamos as alterações necessárias através das seguintes SAP Notes:

  • 3501788 - eSocial - New S-1.3 option for layout configuration
  • 3508286 - eSocial S-1.3 - Transformations objects for standard events without changes
  • 3528070 - Framework - Adjustments to support many employee data in PAL Log output
  • 3525532 - eSocial - Employee Summarizing Events Viewer - Adjustments to use output reporting framework
  • 3517450 - eSocial S-1.3 - Payroll events - S-1200, S-2299 and S-2399
  • 3517449 - eSocial S-1.3 - Transformation objects for payroll events - S-1200, S-2299 and S-2399
  • 3517448 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3517449
  • 3515537 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3515535
  • 3515536 - eSocial S-1.3 - Prerequisite transformation object for SAP Note 3515535
  • 3515535 - eSocial S-1.3 - Table events - S-1000/S-1010
  • 3522167 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3522166
  • 3522166 - eSocial S-1.3 -  Update Visualizador de Eventos Totalizadores de empregador for S-5011 event
  • 3532294 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3531706
  • 3531706 - eSocial S-1.3 - Summarizing Events Viewer - New layout information of S-5001 and S-5002

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você consegue encontrar na SAP Note 3418378 - eSocial - Overview - 2024.

Agora, com a finalização deste projeto, encorajamos todos a revisar as atualizações, familiarizar-se com elas e garantir a conformidade com os novos requisitos em seus próprios sistemas. Destacamos que o ambiente de produção restrita do eSocial já está configurado para envio de eventos na versão S-1.3. Na entrada 2.20 das perguntas frequentes do eSocial você encontra as datas parametrizadas para que seus testes possam ser realizados.

Por fim, é de extrema importância manter seus sistemas atualizados para que a instalação das SAP Notes ocorra de forma mais suave e rápida. Neste link disponibilizamos um documento com os pré-requisitos de Support Package mínimos.

Se tiver alguma dúvida, sugestão ou comentário sobre este post, sinta-se à vontade para compartilhar na seção de comentários abaixo.

Não se esqueça de dar um "like" neste post se você achou útil e compartilhá-lo com seus colegas. Siga a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para se manter atualizado sobre as últimas notícias.

Obrigado por nos acompanhar nesta jornada.

Um abraço,

Anderson Marques

entry.title: Key Updates on Validating a Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment with Rule Functions
entry.content:
You must define rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information for a higher duty or temporary assignment. If these rule functions are not configured, there could be data inconsistencies for the higher duty or temporary assignment, and the higher duty or temporary assignment may not work as intended in the standard solution.

 

Introduction

When you create or edit a higher duty or temporary assignment, you would have defined rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information. In the rule definition for the corresponding rule functions, you can define the validations to be carried out and the validation messages that must be displayed when the validations fail.

If you did not configure these rule functions, there could be data inconsistencies for the higher duty or temporary assignment.

With the higher duty or temporary assignment moving to Centralized Services in Employee Central, these validations occur automatically, whether or not you've configured the rules.

If you had configured the rules, then you only need to disable the rules, to prevent the automatic validations from the centralized services from raising duplicate validation messages. 

However, if you did not configure these rule functions, these validations were not taking place for your higher duty or temporary assignment. With the move to centralized services, these validations will always run in your higher duty solution. To ensure that these validations do not impact your business,  we recommend that you configure the validation rules for your higher duty or temporary assignment and verify that your higher duty solution works with the configured validation rules. This will protect you from any day 1 impact of the higher duty solution moving to centralized services.

For information about the different use cases covered by these validations and how to configure these validation rules, refer to the following:

  • Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Employment Information
    • Define Rule to Validate Employment Information

  • Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Job Information

    •  Define Rule to Validate Job Information

  • Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Compensation Information
    • Define Rule to Validate Compensation Information

 

Summary

To summarize, we saw the importance of defining rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information for a higher duty ore temporary assignment. In the rule definition for the corresponding rule functions, you can define the validations to be carried out and the validation messages that must be displayed when the validations fail.

Configuring these rule functions is very important because these validations will happen in the background when higher duty or temporary assignment runs on centralized services. Not configuring these validations may cause day 1 impact. For more information about centralized services in Employee Central, refer to Centralized Services in Employee Central.

For more details about implementing higher duty or temporary assignment, check out the Implementing Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment guide.

Thank you for reading. I request you to share your feedback by leaving a comment.

entry.title: How to Use and Configure the Transport Center Tool for SuccessFactors: Tips and Tricks
entry.content:
SuccessFactors, SAP's cloud-based Human Capital Management (HCM) suite, offers a variety of tools to streamline HR processes. One key tool within SuccessFactors is the Transport Center Tool, a critical component for managing and migrating configurations across different SuccessFactors environments. Whether you're moving configurations from a development to a test environment, or transferring them to production, mastering the Transport Center is essential for ensuring that your changes are implemented seamlessly.

In this blog post, we'll explore how to use and configure the Transport Center tool for SuccessFactors and provide you with some helpful tips and tricks to make the process more efficient.

What is the Transport Center Tool?

The Transport Center Tool in SuccessFactors is a central hub that facilitates the migration of configurations and customizations across different environments (such as Development, Testing, and Production) within your SuccessFactors landscape. It helps manage changes made to your SuccessFactors instance and ensures that those changes can be transferred safely and efficiently between environments.

Some of the common use cases for the Transport Center include:

  • Moving configuration changes from one environment to another.
  • Transporting metadata, such as employee data models or custom objects.
  • Managing system updates.

The CTC helps maintain consistency and control over your SuccessFactors configuration by offering a structured way to handle these changes.

How to Access the Transport Center Tool in SuccessFactors

Before diving into the configuration of the Transport Center, you first need to access the tool. Here’s how to do that:

  1. Login to SuccessFactors: Use your administrator credentials to log into the SuccessFactors system.
  2. Navigate to the Admin Center: Once logged in, go to the Admin Center by clicking on the "Admin Center" tab in the top menu.
  3. Search for the Transport Center: In the Admin Center, use the search bar to look for the "Transport Center" tool. Click on the search result to open the Transport Center interface.

If you have the necessary permissions, you'll be able to configure and manage the transport jobs from here.

Key Features of the Transport Center Tool

Before configuring the Transport Center, it’s helpful to understand its key features:

  • Transport Jobs: These are the individual tasks that represent a configuration change or set of changes that need to be moved from one environment to another.
  • Import and Export: You can export configuration changes from one environment and import them into another.
  • Package Management: Transport jobs can be grouped into packages for easier management and tracking.
  • Version Control: Transport jobs maintain versioning, making it easy to track changes and revert to previous configurations when necessary.

Step-by-Step Guide to Using the Transport Center Tool

Step 1: Create a Transport Job

Creating a transport job is the first step in using the Transport Center tool. Here's how to create one:

  1. In the Transport Center interface, click on Create Transport Job.
  2. Give your transport job a meaningful name (for example, "Employee Central Config Changes").
  3. Select the Source System (the environment you're exporting from) and the Target System (the environment you're importing to).
  4. Choose the Configuration Type. This could be anything from Employee Central, Recruiting, or Performance Management depending on what you're migrating.
  5. Once the job is created, you’ll need to add the objects or configurations that you want to transport. Click Add Object and select the items you want to include in the job.

Step 2: Export the Transport Job

Once you've created the transport job and added the necessary configurations, it’s time to export it.

  1. In the Transport Center interface, locate the transport job you just created.
  2. Click on Export. This action will prepare the job for migration from the source system to the target system.
  3. Depending on the size of the transport job and the number of configurations, the export process may take a few minutes.

Step 3: Import the Transport Job

After exporting, you’ll need to import the transport job into the target system.

  1. In the Transport Center, switch to the Target System.
  2. Locate the exported transport job under Available Jobs.
  3. Click on Import to begin the transfer process. The system will check the job and ensure all configurations are compatible before proceeding.
  4. Once the import is successful, verify that the configurations are correctly applied in the target system.

Step 4: Test the Transport Job

It’s crucial to test that everything is working as expected after the transport job has been completed. This is especially important in environments like testing or production.

  1. Log into the target environment (for example, the test or production environment).
  2. Check that the configurations, metadata, or custom objects have been correctly applied.
  3. Conduct thorough testing to ensure that everything is functioning as expected. If issues arise, you may need to revise the transport job or troubleshoot any compatibility problems.

Best Practices for Using the Transport Center Tool

While the Transport Center tool is relatively straightforward, here are some best practices to keep in mind for a smooth experience:

1. Use Descriptive Names for Transport Jobs

Give your transport jobs meaningful names that will help you and your team quickly identify what each job does. A clear naming convention (such as "EC_Config_Changes_Dev_to_Test") helps avoid confusion, especially when managing multiple transport jobs. Also try to differentiate fix and enhancements bundles to help the identification of possible bundle errors.

2. Test in a Non-Production Environment

Always test your transport jobs in a non-production environment (such as a sandbox or testing environment) before moving them to production. This helps identify any issues early on and ensures that the configuration works as expected without disrupting live operations.

3. Use Transport Packages

For larger projects, group related transport jobs into packages. This allows you to manage related configurations together and reduces the complexity of transporting them individually. Packages can be version-controlled, making rollback easier if something goes wrong.

4. Monitor Transport Job Status

Keep an eye on the status of your transport jobs to ensure everything is running smoothly. Use the monitoring tools in the CTC to track job progress and quickly identify any issues or errors.

5. Follow Version Control Best Practices

It’s important to keep track of the versions of the configurations you’re transporting. Always document changes and maintain version control to ensure you can easily roll back to a previous version if needed.

6. Caution with release cycles

During the gap between preview and production you may get some errors related to software versions differences, so please plan ahead your transportations

7. Search is tricky

During the search, if you change de filter criteria and click on go, you will not get any info, instead of it, you need to click on the left inside configuration area.

8. Isn't the tool to copy all configurations

You have the limitation on 300 results per search and 50 items per bundle, so if you plan to move a lot of configuration, you will need to break it in several bundles.

9. Attention on RBP copy

If you try to copy groups with filters that point out to data that doesn't exist you will get an error, so be sure you have all the relevant data before copy RBP

 

Troubleshooting Tips

Even with careful planning, issues may arise. Here are some troubleshooting tips for common issues you might encounter:

  • Transport Job Fails: Check the error message and verify that the configuration in the source and target environments is compatible. Sometimes, a mismatch in versions or missing dependencies can cause a transport job to fail.
  • Configuration Not Appearing: If the configuration doesn’t appear in the target environment after the import, verify the transport job’s log for errors and confirm that the import process was completed successfully.
  • Permissions Issues: Ensure you have the appropriate permissions to access and manage the Transport Center. Lack of permissions can prevent you from creating, exporting, or importing transport jobs.

Conclusion

The CTC in SuccessFactors is an essential utility for moving configurations across environments. Whether you're updating a single object or migrating an entire set of configurations, mastering the Transport Center helps ensure that changes are made safely and consistently. By following best practices, naming conventions, and thorough testing, you can avoid common pitfalls and streamline your SuccessFactors configuration management process.

Now that you have a better understanding of how to use and configure the Transport Center tool, you can implement these tips and tricks to maximize its effectiveness and ensure successful migrations every time. Happy transporting!

entry.title: HCM Brasil – DIRF - Ano Base 2024
entry.content:
Olá! 

Bem-vindos ao blog post com as atualizações sobre a DIRF.  
 

A Receita Federal do Brasil divulgou em 14 de novembro de 2024 uma atualização referente à Declaração de Imposto de Renda Retido na Fonte (DIRF) para o ano-base de 2024.  

Anualmente, as empresas necessitam compilar e enviar o arquivo da DIRF às autoridades fiscais. Este arquivo detalha o retorno sobre o imposto de renda que foi retido dos funcionários ao longo do ano. As recentes atualizações introduzidas para o ano-base de 2024 incluem apenas uma mudança técnica: a alteração do Identificador de estrutura do leiaute da DIRF para R6GP3ZA. Como o principal objetivo do leiaute S-1.3 do eSocial é substituir a DIRF, entendemos que essa será a última alteração envolvendo esse relatório legal.  

Entregamos essa mudança na seguinte nota: 3545421 - LC BR - DIRF base year 2024 

As mudanças anunciadas são programadas para entrar em vigência a partir de 01 de janeiro de 2024. 

Para uma análise mais detalhada e para acessar a legislação aplicável, recomendamos consultar o Ato Declaratório Nº 35/2024 publicado pela Receita Federal. 

 

Lembramos que as alterações legais que impactam a localização de folha de pagamento para Brasil sempre são disponibilizadas no Regulatory Change Manager. 

 

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. 

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog Post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag  HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias. 

Um abraço, 
Ana Daudt

entry.title: Employee Business Partner in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Business Partner in SAP S/HANA

SAP Business Partner (BP) is a core functionality in SAP S/4HANA and other SAP systems that enables you to manage and integrate all information related to business partners (such as customers, suppliers, and contacts) in a single structure. It replaces the previously used separate objects, such as Customer Master and Vendor Master, consolidating this information into a more flexible and unified model.

 

 

 

Business Partner in HCM

SAP Business Partner (BP) also plays an important role in the context of HCM (Human Capital Management), especially in systems such as SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA, which integrate HR and finance data.

Using SAP Business Partner in HCM is a strategic solution for unifying employee data, simplifying integrations between HR and finance, and ensuring compliance in people management processes. It is particularly valuable in environments where multiple modules or systems are in use, providing a central point of reference for employee and related partner information.

The technical configuration of SAP Business Partner (BP) for HCM involves several steps. Below, I detail a practical guide to configure and integrate BP with the HCM module:

 

 

Business Partner Category 

  • Person: Natural person, private person.
  • Organization: Legal entity or part of a legal entity, for example, company, department in a company, club, or association.
  • Group: For example, marriage or shared apartment.

Business Partner Grouping

  • Similar to the SAP S/4HANA Finance account group for customers or for vendors
  • Mandatory for each business partner
  • Defined in Customizing
  • Used to assign number ranges (internal/external) to business partners

Business Partner Role

  • Roles define the display and the data of a business partner.
  • SAP S/4HANA provides predefined roles.
  • Customer-specific roles can also be customized.
  • The Business Data Toolset (BDT) is used to influence the GUI.

 

BP Configuration in HCM

  1. Activate BP in SAP S/4HANA:
    • Replace legacy objects (Customer/Vendor Master).
  2. Set Number Ranges:
    • Transaction: BUCG.
    • Example: "Employee BP" (Range: 100000–199999).
  3. Define Roles:
    • Transaction: BUSD.
    • Employee Role: BUP003.
  4. Link to Employee ID:
    • Synchronize with infotypes via transaction PPCI or ALE.

 

There is configuration in SPRO for BP:

SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner

 

Integration with HCM Infotypes

  • Relevant Infotypes:
    • 0001 – Organizational Assignment
    • 0002 – Personal Data
    • 0006 – Address
    • 0009 – Bank details
    • 0015 – Additional Payments

 

  • Tools:
    • HRSFEC_PAO – Synchronization with SuccessFactors
    • SALE – ALE model configuration

 

Practical Example

Creating a Business Partner (BP) for an Employee

Step 1: Create a Number Range for Employees

  • Transaction: BUCG
  • Objective: Set a specific number range for Business Partners related to employees.
  • Example:
    • Group Name: "Employee BP"
    • Number Range: 100000–199999

This ensures each employee gets a unique BP ID within this range.

Step 2: Define Roles for the Business Partner

  • Transaction: BUSD
  • Objective: Assign the appropriate role to the Business Partner (BP).
  • Role for Employees: BUP003 – Employee
  • Description: This role signifies that the BP is related to an employee.

Step 3: Create the Business Partner

  • Transaction: BP
  • Objective: Create the BP and link it to the employee record.
    • Example:
      • Name: Maria Souza
      • BP Role: BUP003 – Employee
      • Address: Rua ABC, 456, São Paulo
      • Employee ID: 12345
      • Link to Infotype: PA0001 (Organizational Assignment)
      • Number: 100001 (assigned from the range defined in Step 1)

Step 4: Synchronize Data with HCM

  • Transaction: PPCI (HCM Master Data Synchronization)
  • Objective: Link employee data to the BP and synchronize relevant infotypes such as:
    • 0001: Organizational Assignment
    • 0002: Personal Data
    • 0006: Address
    • 0009: Bank details

Step 5: Test the BP Integration

  • Objective: Ensure that data is correctly synchronized between BP and the employee record.
    • Test Case: Check if the BP record is reflected correctly in the HCM system and can be accessed in other modules like payroll (FI) or materials management (MM) when required.

Outcome:

  • Employee Maria Souza now has a unique BP record connected to her HCM data.
  • The BP is properly configured with the correct role and data synchronization.
  • The BP can now be used across other modules, ensuring consistency and integration in the SAP system.

 

Useful Tools and Transactions

Transaction

Description

BP

Create and maintain Business Partner records.

BUCG

Set up number ranges for BP.

BUSD

Manage BP roles.

PPCI

Configure infotype integration.

HRSFEC_PAO

Synchronize BP with SuccessFactors master data.

SALE

Configure ALE distribution model.

Final Considerations

  • Perform rigorous testing to ensure data consistency and integration.
  • Implement security controls to protect sensitive data.
  • Monitor audit logs to track BP record changes.

 

This blog post aims to help you quickly and concisely understand the Business Partner setup for HCM in S/4 HANA. The steps mentioned will help you understand the activities that need to be performed during the setup.

If you need more information about the new employee business partner model in S/4 HANA, please visit the following links:

 New_Employee_Business_Partner_Model_in_S4HANA.pdf

SAP Note 2578294 - FAQ - Employee to Business Partner synchronization in SAP S/4HANA OnPremise 

New Business Partner Model

Business Partner Apps

 

I hope this helps for a quick understanding of the new Employee Business Partner in SAP S/4 HANA

Best Regards

entry.title: Novedades Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones - Formulario 220
entry.content:
Do you want to read this blog in English? Go to Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones Updates – 220 Form 

Hola a todos, 

Con este blog les queremos traer las novedades que tenemos sobre el formulario 220 Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones (CIR). El propósito es tener bien informados a nuestros clientes sobre las actualizaciones de este reporte. 

Contexto

En estos últimos años la Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales (DIAN) ha venido solicitando información más detallada en el reporte CIR. Estas informaciones se refieren a algunos pagos o contribuciones específicos. A continuación, explicaremos cuales han sido los cambios en los últimos años.

 

Requerimientos

Ahorro programado para la construcción:

En el año 2023 (año gravable 2022) se incluyó en el reporte CIR los ahorros programados de Ahorro Voluntario Contractual (AVC). Este ahorro voluntario se refiere a los empleados que tienen cuenta con el Fondo Nacional del Ahorro (FNA). Para el año 2023 esta información se debía presentar en la misma línea con los aportes de las cuentas de Ahorro para el Fomento de la Construcción (AFC). Para el año 2024 (año gravable 2023) la DIAN determino que esta información debería ir separada de la siguiente manera: 

  • En la línea 57 se deben reportar los “Aportes a cuentas AFC”, correspondientes a todos los aportes AFC excepto FNA. 
  • En la línea 58 se deben reportar “Aportes a cuentas AVC”, correspondientes a las deducciones que se haya realizado al trabajador para el FNA. 

Cesantías:

Antes del año 2024 los pagos por cesantías se presentaban en una misma línea para empleados con relación laboral Régimen Tradicional y con relación laboral Ley50, y las consignaciones a fondos de cesantías se presentaban en otra línea diferente. La DIAN para el año 2024 (año gravable 2023) realizo un cambio. Para este año las cesantías se deben presentar de la siguiente manera: 

  • En la línea 47, se deben reportar los pagos por “Auxilio de cesantía e intereses efectivamente pagados al empleado”. 
  • En la línea 48, se deben reportar “Auxilio de cesantía reconocido a trabajadores del régimen tradicional del CST, contenido en el Capítulo VII, Título VIII Parte Primera”. 
  • En la línea 49, se deben reportar “Auxilio de cesantía consignado al fondo de cesantías”. 

Bonos de alimentación: 

Otro cambio incluido en el año 2024 se refiere a los pagos por alimentación y pagos realizados con bonos electrónicos o de papel, que también deben ser reportados de forma separada de la siguiente manera:

  • En la línea 37 se deben reportar “Pagos realizados con bonos electrónicos o de papel de servicio, cheques, tarjetas, vales, etc.“, los bonos de alimentación que no exceden las 41 UVT y donde el salario mensual del empleado sea superior a 310 UVT.
  • En la línea 38 se deben reportar “Valor del exceso de los pagos por alimentación mayores a 41 UVT, art. 3871 E.T.:” los bonos de alimentación donde el salario mensual del empleado no supere las 310 UVT y el bono de alimentación sea superior a 41 UVT, este exceso constituye ingreso tributario del trabajador, sometido a retención en la fuente por ingresos laborales.

 

Solución

El desafío que tuvimos con estos nuevos requerimientos de la DIAN, es que esta información solicitada no existe de forma natural en la nómina, es decir, no existen CC-nóminas específicas dentro de la nómina para ese fin. A continuación, mencionamos la solución que hemos entregado para cada uno de los requerimientos:

Aportes a cuentas AVC:

Con el fin de cubrir esta solicitud, creamos un nuevo aporte voluntario dentro de la nómina. Este nuevo aporte voluntario es exclusivamente usado para cuentas AVC. De esta manera ya se tienen desde la nómina, CC-nóminas independientes donde cada cliente puede parametrizarlas de acuerdo con su interpretación. Si desea más información la siguiente nota SAP contiene la solución:
3457085 - [CO] New voluntary contribution to Contractual Voluntary Savings accounts .


Cesantías:

Para cubrir esta solicitud decidimos crear nuevas CC-nóminas las cuales son generadas en el cálculo de la nómina, y se crean en el momento que el empleado tiene pago de anticipos de cesantías o de intereses de cesantías. Estas nuevas CC-nóminas son informativas y son generadas exclusivamente para ser configuradas en el reporte CIR. Con esta solución es más fácil y flexible la configuración de cesantías para el reporte CIR. Si desea más información la siguiente nota SAP contiene esta solución:
3464714 – [CO] Severance – New wages types for CIR report .

Bonos de Alimentación:

La solución que encontramos para los bonos de alimentación fue la misma que usamos para las cesantías, es decir: crear CC-nóminas desde la nómina para cubrir esta necesidad. Estas nuevas CC-nóminas serán informativas, es decir, no tendrán una incidencia directa en el cálculo del neto del empleado y serán exclusivas para que sean configuradas en el reporte CIR, de esta manera cada cliente podrá configurar el reporte CIR de acuerdo con sus interpretaciones. 

Si desea más información las siguientes notas SAP contienen esta solución: 
3459605 - [CO] TAX - Calculating new wage types for food voucher. 
3537740 - [CO] TAX - New wage type for food voucher higher than the maximum deducible value. 

 

Pasos a seguir

Es muy importante que nuestros clientes tengan implementadas estas notas a más tardar a finales de diciembre de 2024, de esta manera las nuevas CC-nominas se generaran desde el primer periodo de enero del 2025, y serán almacenadas en los resultados de nómina, con esto, el reporte CIR del año gravable 2025 (presentado en el 2026) se generara con todos los valores leídos desde los resultados de la nómina. 

En cuanto a la información del año gravable 2024 que se presentara en el 2025, el reporte CIR simulará estas nuevas CC-nominas durante la ejecución del reporte y los incluirá en el formulario de acuerdo con la configuración que el cliente haya hecho en los acumuladores respectivos. Es importante tener en cuenta, para que el reporte simule estas nuevas CC-nóminas, estas deberán existir, es decir, las notas mencionadas anteriormente deben estar implementadas en el sistema del cliente. 

 

Gracias por formar parte de esta comunidad.

 

Omar Granados Mateus 
Tech Lead para America Latina, SAP Globalization

entry.title: Batch Upload Germany (DE) – Double file upload & Semi-Colon format.
entry.content:
Introduction:

Batch upload is done in Germany in different form compared to regular txt upload. Recording with txt formats are common but Batch upload using program is an alternate way to upload which we will see in detail in this blog. 

Object Attributes Set up:

Instead of recording, Program can be developed and used to define object attributes similar to screenshot below. Programmers can assist you based on the requirement. Below program is to upload Licenses of employees in 2 different files(Header & Content)

Source Structure & Source Field set up:

This source structure and source field are entirely unique unlike regular forms of LSMW as it involves two structures Header and Content which will be processed in 2 different files in the same batch upload.

Head & Item:

Header and Contents are divided as below as they will be processed in separate files in the same LSMW. Validation of header and content format is important as well as upload will fail in case of any discrepancy in the file format.

Source Relations and Field Mapping:

Auto field mapping is enabled using the mapping option and saving the settings similar to regular configuration process. Click the required mapping and press "ok" to save them. This step is important to map the fields. Any discrepancy or missed will lead to upload issues.

Default settings Modified:

Default setting modified as below for this unique batch upload. Regular settings are used for normal upload using recording but in this case of double file CSV format upload following changes are required.

Header Content:

Header Fields are displayed as per the upload format. Match the header fields provided by the customer are enabled correctly in this step to make sure its not missed.

Header(Based on requirement)

Additional fields are based on the requirement can be used or unused during upload process. Better keep the minimum fields required for successful upload and unwanted fields can be removed.

Header(Based on requirement)

Additional fields based on the requirement can be used or unused during upload process. Keep it to minimum mandate required fields only.

Source Field Set Up:

Source Fields are set us a below with accordance to the fields being used in the upload process. This will display the final set of fields, technical names, characters which will help to analyze the upload creation and proceed to further steps.

Fields based on the requirement:

Additional fields can be used or unused based on the upload requirement for Finance. Keep it to minimum and remove unnecessary fields which are not processed.

Instruction Key and Batch Input field.

Instruction key and batch input field set up based on the requirement and upload information by the system. This is set by default by the system based on the above steps performed.

Fixed Values ,Translations, User-Defined Routines:

Fixed values, Translations, user definition will reflect by default no major changes required. Just click and save this step before going ahead with specify files.

Header and Content file upload:

In Specify files, this programmed batch upload is designed to upload 2 separate files unlike normal LSMW Batch upload. Open the files and check the format, header and content before attaching them as this step is crucial.

Header and Content File Upload:

Tabulator option used in general but this unique upload uses semi-Colon. File format will have fields in semicolon format unlike regular txt format based on it settings below are selected.

Content and Fields Upload:

Upload process as usual but the difference is 2 different files are used. Header saved and post which content file is saves in 2 different steps inside Specify files.

Validate the upload file to check any space or format issue exists before upload.

Files received from the customer are bound to have errors like spacing, lining and missing information. It requires complete validation and revert back to the customer to send the updated format in case of any errors

File uploaded in CSV format:

Unlike txt format used in the regular upload, Here we can directly upload using CSV file format for this set up and program.

Perform regular Read, Display, Convert & Display Converted data steps similar to regular batch upload.

No change in these steps as they follow usual protocol which is self explanatory.

Now, Click on the last step "Start Direct Input Program".

Select the program below.

Execute upload by using the program and options below including log:

Log and Batch upload(B) option to be selected before executing.

Go to SM35 and check the log:

Log result can be tracked in SM35 with successful records along with the processed time. This can be used for audit purpose as well.

Conclusion:

Batch upload Processed successfully. Perform it in Quality system and get a quality assurance from the client. Post confirmation from the client, go ahead with Production.

entry.title: Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones Updates - 220 Form
entry.content:
¿Quieres leer este blog en español? ¡Accede a  Novedades Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones – Formulario 220.

 

Hello everyone, 

This blog aims to bring you the latest news on the 220 form Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones (CIR), to keep our customers informed on this report’s updates and changes. 

 

Context 

It’s been some years that the Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales (DIAN) has been requesting more detailed information on the CIR report. This information relates to some specific payments and contributions. Next, we explain what the changes have been in recent years. 

 

Requirements 

Ahorro programado para la construcción (Scheduled contribution for construction): 

In the year 2023 (tax year 2022), the scheduled contributions of Contractual Voluntary Contribution (Ahorro Voluntario Contractual - AVC) were included in the CIR report. This voluntary contribution refers to employees who have an account with the National Savings Fund (Fondo Nacional del Ahorro - FNA). For the year 2023 this information should be presented in the same line with the contributions from the savings accounts for the Contribution to Construction Fomentation (Ahorro Para Fomento de la Construcción - AFC). For the year 2024 (taxable year 2023) the DIAN determined that this information should be separated as follows:

  • Line 57 must present the “Aportes a cuentas AFC” (Contributions to AFC accounts), corresponding to all AFC contributions, except for FNA. 
  • Line 58 must present the “Aportes a cuentas AVC” (Contributions to AVC accounts), corresponding to the deductions that have been made to the employee for FNA. 

Cesantías (Severance Payments): 

Before the year 2024, severance payments were presented on the same line for employees under the Régimen Tradicional work regime and under the Ley50 work regime, and consignments to severance funds were presented in a different line. For the year 2024 (taxable year 2023), DIAN has made some changes. With that, severance fees must be presented as follows: 

  • Line 47 must report “Auxilio de cesantía e intereses efectivamente pagados al empleado” (payments for severance and interest effectively paid to the employee). 
  • Line 48 must report “Auxilio de cesantía reconocido a trabajadores del régimen tradicional del CST” (payments to employees belonging to the CST Régimen Tradicional work regime), according to Chapter VII, title VIII, first part. 
  • Line 49 must report “Auxilio de cesantía consignado al fondo de cesantías” (severance payment registered to the severance fund). 

Bonos de alimentación (Food vouchers): 

Another change included in the year of 2024 refers to food and electronic or paper voucher payments, which must also be reported separately as follows: 

  • Line 37 must report “Pagos realizados con bonos electrónicos o de papel de servicio, cheques, tarjetas, vales, etc.” (Payments made with electronic or paper vouchers, checks, cards, vouchers, etc). Food vouchers that do not exceed 41 UVT and where the monthly salary of the employee is greater than 310 UVT. 
  • Line 38 must report “Valor del exceso de los pagos por alimentación mayores a 41 UVT, art. 3871 E.T.:” (“Excess amount of food payments greater than 41 UVT, Art. 3871 E.T.). Food vouchers where the employee’s monthly salary does not exceed 310 UVT and the food voucher is greater than 41 UVT, this excess amount constitutes the employee’s tax income, subject to tax withheld at source for employment income. 

 

Solution: 

The challenge with these new DIAN requirements is that this requested information does not exist naturally in the payroll, that is, there are no specific wage types within the payroll for this purpose. Below, we mention the solution that we have delivered for each one of the requirements: 

Contributions to AVC Accounts: 

In order to cover this application, we create a new voluntary contribution within the payroll. This new voluntary contribution is exclusively used for AVC accounts. This way, independent wage types are already available in the payroll, where the customer can set them up according to their needs. For more information, check the following SAP Note:
3457085 - [CO] New voluntary contribution to Contractual Voluntary Savings accounts.

 
Cesantías (Severance Payments): 

For this request we decide to create new wage types, which are generated in the payroll calculation, and are created at the time the employee has advance severance payment or interests over severance payment. These new wage types are informative and are generated exclusively to be configured in the CIR report. 

With this solution, the configuration of severance benefits for the CIR report is easier and more flexible. For more information, check the following SAP Note:
3464714 – [CO] Severance – New wages types for CIR report. 


Food vouchers: 

For food vouchers the solution is the same we used for severance payments, that is, to create wage types in the payroll calculation. These new wage types will be informative and will not have a direct impact on the payroll calculation, being exclusively configured in the CIR report. This way, the customer can configure the CIR report according to their needs. 
For more information, check the following SAP Notes: 
3459605 - [CO] TAX - Calculating new wage types for food voucher.  
3537740 - [CO] TAX - New wage type for food voucher higher than the maximum deductible value. 

 

Next Steps: 

Now, it is esencial that our customers have these notes implemented by the end of December 2024, in this way the new wage types will be generated from the first period of January 2025, and will be stored in the payroll results, with this, the CIR report of taxable year 2025 (presented in 2026) will be generated with all values read from the payroll results. 

Regarding the information for taxable year 2024, to be submitted in 2025, the CIR report will simulate these new wage types during the report run and include them in the form according to the configuration the customer has made in the respective accumulators. It is important to take into account, that in order to the report to simulates these new wage types, these must exist, that is, the notes mentioned above must be implemented in the customer system. 

 

Thank you for being part of this community. 

 

Omar Granados Mateus
Tech Lead para America Latina, SAP Globalization

entry.title: How to master a successful on-premise H4S4 Migration: SAP ERP6 HCM to SAP HCM for S/4HANA (H4S4)
entry.content:
1         Introduction

The current on-premise solution, SAP ERP Human Capital Management (HCM), will only be available until the end of 2027 (with extended maintenance until 2030). The so-called compatibility mode of SAP HCM, which applies to integrated installations on an S/4HANA system, will expire on December 31, 2025 (see SAP Notes 3443536 and 269324). Consequently, the corresponding usage rights will end on January 1, 2026. Since the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release, Human Capital Management for S/4HANA (H4S4) has been an integral part of S/4HANA.

2         Motivation

Based on our discussions with various companies and stakeholders at DSAG Leipzig 2024, it became clear that transitioning to the successor solution, SAP HCM for S/4HANA (H4S4), presents a significant challenge for many SAP customers. Having successfully completed our own migration to H4S4 in November 2024, we want to share our insights and demonstrate how you can also master this transition effectively and achieve success.

3         The phases of our migration project

Our migration project was structured into the following phases, which will be discussed in detail below:

  1. Definition of the IT and HR Roadmap
  2. Checking the prerequisites for Human Capital Management for S/4HANA
  3. Analyzing the legacy SAP ERP HCM system
  4. Preparing and getting the SAP ERP HCM system S/4HANA ready
  5. Performing the first “dirty migration” to S/4HANA
  6. Testing and analyzing the migrated system
  7. Fixing functional gaps, custom code etc.
  8. Performing the migration of the development system
  9. Doing further adaptions
  10. Performing the QAS system migration
  11. Performing the main tests with HR business team
  12. Performing the productive System migration
  13. Hypercare & follow-up

The phases were aligned with the SAP Activate Methodology, which provided a structured framework for the migration process. In retrospect, this phased approach was essential for managing the complexities of the migration and ensuring a smooth transition to SAP HCM for S/4HANA. We will now describe each phase in detail to highlight the key activities and challenges involved.

3.1        Definition of the IT and HR-Roadmap

The first step in your migration journey is to define a clear IT and HR roadmap. This includes determining your target system landscape and setting realistic timelines. Without a well-thought-out roadmap, achieving success will be significantly more challenging.

In our case, we were operating a standalone SAP ERP 6.0 system with EHP8 and the latest service pack level. Given the end of maintenance for SAP ERP and an upcoming HR transformation project, we decided to move to S/4HANA with H4S4 to adopt the latest SAP HCM solution.

Migrating completely to SAP SuccessFactors was not a viable option for us due to our complex billing and time models, which are not fully supported by SuccessFactors. Additionally, we were satisfied with our on-premises environment, which ensured data sovereignty and met our compliance requirements. As a result, migrating to a cloud-based solution was not considered a suitable alternative.

It is worth noting that there are often rumors suggesting that only cloud-based solutions represent a future-proof investment, as on-premises solutions are sometimes perceived as outdated. However, SAP has repeatedly affirmed its commitment to supporting S/4HANA on-premises systems until at least 2040, as emphasized by Thomas Saueressig at DSAG Leipzig 2024. SAP has also confirmed that there will be ongoing innovations for on-premises solutions, even though certain technologies will remain exclusive to the cloud.

Summary:

With these considerations in mind, we finalized our IT and HR roadmap, firmly deciding on H4S4 as our on-premises solution. This clarity allowed us to confidently embark on our migration journey.

3.2        Checking the prerequisites for Human Capital Management for S/4HANA

SAP Note 3091160 (SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA – General Approach and Strategy) serves as the central reference and entry point for the migration to H4S4. This was our starting point for the journey, and we strongly recommend considering it as part of your preparation.

H4S4 is built on the foundation of SAP ERP HCM, offering a comparable functional scope and supporting similar business processes. It runs natively on SAP HANA, which delivers significant performance benefits. However, H4S4 introduces only minimal changes; it essentially uses the same code base. Details about updates and differences can be found in SAP Notes such as 3091160. Below are a few examples:

Personnel Time Management (PT):

  • Note 3138281 - Functions no longer available in time evaluation
    • Certain infotypes are now obsolete.
    • Some operations have been modified.
    • A number of reports have been updated.
    • ...
  • Note 3164668 - New Functions in the Time Management Environment
    • Time evaluation (RPTIME00): Enhanced error analysis and the ability to skip "incorrect" personnel numbers in the evaluation log.
    • Collision check of time infotypes: Simplified and more flexible customization options, with the restriction to a maximum of 50 entries removed.
    • ...
  • Note 3139908 - Functions no longer available in time data recording
    • Note 3429460: Adjustments to the menu bar and application toolbar in transaction PA61.
    • Note 3346202: Updates to the Time Management Pool (transaction PT40).

General Updates:

  • Introduction of new MSS/ESS apps.
  • Licensing for H4S4 is required (Note 3200799 - Business Function for SAP Human Capital Management for SAP S/4HANA).
  • Obsolete development objects need to be checked and, if still in use, replaced.
  • ...and several other minor adjustments.

Summary:

We found the changes in H4S4 to be minimal, which enabled us to carry out the migration independently with our internal IT team.

3.3        Analyzing the legacy SAP ERP HCM system

Before starting any concrete migration steps, it is essential to analyze your system thoroughly. This step helps you understand what lies ahead and what specific challenges the migration may entail.

System analysis can be divided into two key parts:

  1. Analyzing the overall system:
  • Assessing the general technical and infrastructural readiness for S/4HANA.
  • Reviewing database compatibility, hardware requirements, and SAP Notes for system upgrades.
  1. Analyzing the HCM components in use:
  • Identifying all active modules, custom developments, and integrations in SAP ERP HCM.
  • Evaluating dependencies and critical processes that must be preserved in the new environment.

The most critical aspect of the entire migration process is ensuring your SAP ERP system is S/4HANA-ready. Without achieving this general readiness, the HCM migration to H4S4 is destined to fail. Thorough preparation at this stage will lay the foundation for a successful transition.

3.3.1        Analyzing the general system

To prepare our system for S/4HANA, we conducted the Simplification Item Check (report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK) and the Readiness Check (report RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA). Both checks are mandatory steps to identify the tasks required to ensure S/4HANA readiness, as previously mentioned.

In addition, we analyzed modifications and changes to standard objects using SE95, with the goal of adopting a clean core approach. This step was critical to minimize custom code dependencies and simplify future updates.

We reviewed SPAU and SPDD to address any unresolved adjustments from prior migrations or upgrades. For our custom code, we used the ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC), leveraging the S4HANA_READINESS_2023 check variant alongside our custom-specific checks to ensure compatibility with S/4HANA.

Finally, we assessed the compatibility of implemented SAP and third-party add-ons using the Readiness Check to identify any necessary updates or replacements.

3.3.2        Analyzing the HCM component

We also utilized the Readiness Check and Simplification Item Check to identify HCM components and items requiring special attention during the migration. These tools provided a structured overview of critical areas, such as obsolete functionality, required adjustments, and technical dependencies.

Additionally, as part of our preparation, we meticulously reviewed all related SAP Notes referenced in the general note 3091160. This included a detailed manual review to identify obsolete infotypes, objects, reports, and other components that might impact our processes. This step was particularly important to ensure nothing critical was overlooked, especially in areas not automatically flagged by the tools.

Key Takeaways from System Analysis:

  1. Awareness of Project Scope:
    Analyzing the system provided us with a comprehensive understanding of the project's scope and highlighted potential complexities.
  2. Effort Estimation:
    This step served as the foundation for estimating the effort required for the migration. If your management requests an estimate early in the project, this phase is the ideal opportunity to gather the necessary data.
  3. Planning Next Steps:
    The insights gained allowed us to outline the subsequent tasks and prioritize areas that required immediate attention, such as addressing obsolete objects or verifying third-party add-ons.

Effort Estimation Guidance:

With the information collected during this phase, we conducted a detailed S/4HANA H4S4 effort estimation. This process was informed by the methodology described in the SAP Community blog: A method to estimate the effort of a transformation project (SAP S/4HANA) especially the Custom Code Migration

Using this approach, we were able to calculate the necessary resources for both technical and functional areas, providing clear input for project planning and decision-making.

3.4        Preparing and getting the SAP ERP HCM system S/4HANA ready

Analyzing the system served as our entry point to ensure it was ready for S/4HANA. With all the necessary information gathered, we began the adaptation phase.

General System Tasks:

We addressed the following tasks:

  • Inconsistencies from the Simplification Item Check:
    • We identified various inconsistencies, most of which could be easily resolved.
  • Simplification Items that needed Attention (e.g., Business Partners):
    • Since we did not use customers, vendors, or employees as business partners in the standalone system, we decided to archive (i.e., delete) them and not to migrate to CVI / BP.
  • Unnecessary Modifications:
    • We removed obsolete modifications and for example moved any remaining necessary ones to implicit enhancements.
  • SPAU / SPDD Leftovers:
    • We cleared any leftovers from previous migrations or upgrades in SPAU and SPDD.
  • Findings in the ATC Check:
    • We addressed the issues identified during the ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) check, including custom code and compatibility adjustments.

HCM Component Tasks:

For the HCM components, we focused on:

  • Adapting Schemes and Rules
    • We updated and customized the relevant time evaluation schemes and rules to be compatible with H4S4.
  • Migrating IT Infotypes
    • We migrated obsolete infotypes to the actual ones.

Summary:

The inconsistencies reported by the Simplification Item Check must be resolved in every system to ensure a smooth migration to S/4HANA. Addressing these items early in the process is crucial for a successful transition.

3.5        Performing the first “dirty migration” to S/4HANA

Once the general system tasks were completed, we set up a separate system to perform the first - we called it - "dirty S/4HANA migration". The goal was to test whether our previous adjustments were sufficient to successfully migrate the system.

We successfully carried out the first SPAU / SPDD check on the S/4HANA shadow system, which allowed us to draw valuable conclusions from the comparison. Some objects still required further adaptation, but overall, the migration process was on track.

Long story short, we were able to successfully migrate the system.

Summary:

Performing a "dirty migration" to S/4HANA helps validate the previous adaptations and actions, ensuring the migration process is feasible and identifying any remaining issues early on

3.6        Testing and analyzing the migrated system

After the migration, we carried out the standard post-upgrade steps (e.g., RZ10, SACF, SU25, activating Fiori) to identify any additional issues that needed to be addressed. We repeated the checks outlined in section 3.3 to ensure everything was in order.

Following this, the Human Resources department was involved in two key areas: first, to familiarize themselves with the S/4HANA system, and second, to perform Payroll (PY) and Personnel Time (PT) billing. They also reviewed the t-codes and reports in use.

During this process, we identified a few minor adjustments that were still needed for the schemes and cycles. Additionally, we found that the bank data (table T012) needed to be migrated manually.

3.7        Fixing functional gaps, custom code etc.

In this phase, we carried out the next adaptation cycle based on the findings from the previous phase.

3.8        Performing the migration of the development system

During further adjustments and fixes, we realized that the development system now needed to be migrated to S/4HANA, as certain adaptations could only be made within an S/4HANA environment. These requirements were primarily identified during the "dirty migration" of the S/4HANA system.

Before migrating the development system, we conducted another dirty migration to validate our findings and familiarize ourselves with the SUM/SL-Toolset steps.

As expected, the migration of the development system was completed successfully, without encountering any major issues.

Summary:

During the adaptation process, we identified the need to migrate the development system to S/4HANA. To ensure a smooth migration, we performed another "dirty migration" to validate the changes and familiarize ourselves with the tools. The migration of the development system was ultimately successful, with no significant problems.

3.9        Doing further adaptions

As mentioned earlier, we were now able to perform the S/4HANA-specific steps, such as SPAU/SPDD, in the development system. Additionally, we imposed a soft system freeze on the QAS and production systems. You might wonder why it was only a soft system freeze. The reason is that there were no major issues with transporting objects like schemes, cycles, customizing (if the relevant tables already existed), and custom code to the systems still running SAP ERP. Yes, you may encounter warning symbols (because of different nametabs / columns) in STMS during the transport, but it still works.

To clarify, we re-imported all objects and transport requests that generated warnings during the import, after the production system was migrated to ensure everything was functioning correctly.

In essence, our soft system freeze involved a restriction on transporting SAP standard objects and implemented SAP Notes. Furthermore, every transport to QAS and production had to be reviewed and approved by the STMS owner, but we did not generally block any transport.

3.10    Performing the QAS system migration

After completing all the known adaptations, we migrated the QAS system to S/4HANA. As expected, the migration proceeded smoothly without any major issues. In the meantime, S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 was released with a new SUM version. We encountered a few minor issues due to bugs introduced in FPS02 and newest SUM version, but we were able to resolve them internally.

3.11    Performing the main tests with HR business team

In this step, the Human Resources (HR) department was re-engaged to test the core HR processes. As described in section 3.6, it was now easier for the HR team to perform these tests, as they were already familiar with the question, "What should we test?" The testing phase was more efficient due to their hands-on experience with the system and the previously identified areas of focus.

The tests covered a wide range of processes, including payroll (PY), personnel time (PT), and employee data management. Overall, there were minimal issues reported, and most of the processes worked as expected. Any minor issues that did arise were quickly addressed by the project team.

With the successful completion of these tests, we were confident that the system was ready for the final productive migration.

3.12    Performing the productive System migration

In coordination with the Human Resources department, we set the migration date. As anticipated, the migration was successful and proceeded without any major issues.

3.13    Hypercare & follow-up

Afterward, we performed the necessary steps, including implementing transport requests (SU25, SACF, Fiori activation). We also updated the system parameters to the recommended values as outlined in the SAP Security Guide. Additionally, we reviewed our processes to ensure functionality.

Due to the activation of SACF scenarios, we encountered some new authorization checks, which led to minor authorization issues. These were resolved quickly and efficiently.

4         Issues and Lessons learned

  • We encountered some minor issues with inconsistent documents (e.g. FI) while archiving customers and vendors. As a result, we had to resolve and remove these inconsistent documents.
  • As previously mentioned, S/4HANA FPS02 caused issues initially. We began the migration on QAS the day after FPS02 was released, along with the newest version of SUM. During the process, we faced inconsistencies with SAP standard objects that were not delivered successfully, and SUM also had some minor issues.
  • After the productive migration, we noticed that the wage type texts had reverted to their standard values. We manually corrected them, but in hindsight, we should have backed them up using standard reports (RPU12W0S / RPU12W0C).
  • Additionally, the system settings in table T77S0 (part of SAP) were altered during the upgrade, and we needed to restore them according to our specific requirements. It’s crucial to back up this table beforehand.
  • If SAP Connector for Microsoft .NET (NCo) is used, it needs to be updated to the latest version.
  • If Travel Management in SAP HCM system is used, be aware about note 2906550 - New Interface between SAP Travel Management and FI: activity based costing and earmarked funds. Implement the notes on ERP side, otherwise you aren't able to post the travel expenses to ERP. 

5         Key Success Factors

  •   Early Definition of the IT and HR Roadmap
    An early and clear definition of the IT and HR roadmap was crucial for the project’s success. The roadmap ensured that all relevant milestones, dependencies, and timelines were outlined, allowing the project to progress along a clear and structured path from the very beginning.
  •   System Analysis as a Basis for Informed Decisions
    Comprehensive analysis of the existing system made us aware of necessary adjustments and potential challenges early on. This helped us realistically estimate the effort, derive concrete measures, and prioritize tasks effectively.
  •   Phase-Based Migration Approach
    Dividing the migration into clearly defined phases proved to be ideal. This step-by-step approach minimized risks, identified problems early, and ensured a systematic migration process.
  •   Strong S/4HANA Migration Expertise in the IT Team
    The IT team’s extensive knowledge and experience with S/4HANA migration were key success factors. Their expertise enabled quick resolution of technical challenges and ensured an efficient migration process.
  •   Close Collaboration Between IT and HR Teams
    Close and continuous collaboration between the IT and HR teams was essential. Regular exchange, thorough testing, and the active involvement of the HR department ensured that all requirements were met and processes were migrated seamlessly.
  •   Thorough Testing and Validation
    Conducting extensive testing ensured that all critical processes—especially in Payroll (PY) and Time Management (PT)—worked as expected. Issues were identified and resolved in time, enabling a stable go-live.
  •   Clear Communication with All Stakeholders
    Transparent and continuous communication with all stakeholders created a shared understanding of the project goals and progress. This helped build trust, avoid misunderstandings, and ensure broad acceptance of the project.

6         Summary

We successfully migrated to Human Capital Management on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02.
For a successful system migration, performing the Simplification Item Check and the Readiness Check is mandatory. However, these checks alone are not sufficient. Carefully removing modifications and meticulously processing SPAU and SPDD are critical factors that can make or break the migration.

Additionally, leveraging other S/4HANA migration tools such as ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit), SCMON, SCOV, and others is equally important. These tools should not be overlooked, as they provide valuable informations and guidance for ensuring a smooth migration.

For the successful migration of SAP ERP HCM to H4S4, a key success factor was systematically working through the relevant SAP Notes. By addressing and implementing these notes proactively, we were able to resolve tasks and adjust the system before the first migration.

Note: If you have any questions, feel free to ask in the comments. For further discussion, don’t hesitate to reach out to me via my LinkedIn profile.

entry.title: Germany – Batch upload Error and Solution
entry.content:
Introduction:

German batch upload can enter different phase of errors during the process. Its important to analyze the potential errors and process the upload.

Upload Error:

During the batch upload process in LSMW, following error pops up Source Structure and field mismatch while executing read data.

Error Analysis based on error description:

As per the error description, we will check source fields are content file sequence is matching.

If Fields are not matching, please check with the requestor.

If Fields are matching, we will move to the next step of analysis.

Step1:

German batch upload is processed in CSV format with Semi-colon. We have to check the file format is CSV.

We are uploading 2 files Header and item.

In the file received, we can clearly check the format above.

Header file in the example above is clearly the correct CSV format sent by the requestor. Contrastingly, Item file is not in the correct format (Microsoft excel worksheet).

In this case, we always send it back to the requestor to recheck and share the correct format for accuracy.

Step 2:

File format for both Header and Item file are accurate, but still, we get the same error.

Correct format:

Error:

In this case, we have to deep dive into the file and check the fields inside are aligned correctly with the upload format.

Header File Requestor format:

Batch upload Correct Format:

Format for header file is correctly matching the upload format. No issues observer here. If the format is mismatching send the correct sample format to the requestor for correction in the file.

Step 3:

We will check the item file field format to identify the potential formatting issue as header file has no error.

Item file format sent by the requestor:

Correct Batch Upload format:

In the comparison above, we can clearly see the format sent by the requestor is incorrect. Cells and values are separated by the requestor which is causing the error.  

Conclusion:

Based on 3 step analysis, it’s clear the file format and the value format sent by the requestor for item file is not accurate. Its important to understand requestors perspective and share the correct sample file to them as they may not expertise the upload process done by consultants. Once requestor reviews and sends the correct format. We are good to go for batch upload successfully.

entry.title: HCM Brasil - 13º Salário Maternidade Adoção - Pagamento efetuado pelo INSS
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

No dia 3 de janeiro de 2025 foram entregues duas SAP Note referentes ao envio para o eSocial dos valores de 13º salário maternidade adoção que foram pagos pelo INSS.

Com essa solução, temos uma nova rubrica de caráter informativa (/362 - 13º lic.mat. INSS eSocial) que contêm especificamente a parte paga pelo INSS dos valores de 13º salário maternidade adoção para o reporte ao eSocial.

Nesta entrega as seguintes SAP Notes foram liberadas:

  • 3483047 - Maternity leave 13th salary - Payment by INSS.
  • 3483598 - eSocial - S-1010 - Maternity adoption 13th salary - Payment by INSS.

Importante

Essa nova rubrica tem seu valor baseado na rubrica /349 (13º Licença Matern.), seu valor é acumulado na /142 (Base FGTS (13o)) e é gerada apenas nos cálculos de folha de 1313 e 13DI.

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você consegue encontrar na SAP Note 3556989 - eSocial - Overview - 2025.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias.

Um abraço,

Anderson Bueno

entry.title: Maximizing the Value of AI for HR
entry.content:
Organizations today recognize the potential of AI to improve their business processes and workforce outcomes, but they can only realize AI’s promised value if their workforce understands and effectively adopts new AI tools. To understand the current state of AI at work – and in particular, HR – the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team hosted interviews with 79 HR and IT professionals from 59 SAP SuccessFactors customer organizations and conducted a global survey of 4,023 individual contributors and people managers across regions and industries. We have taken insights from the research and highlighted strategic and technological recommendations for organizations to capitalize on the potential of AI. 

The first report, "HR’s guide to enterprise AI adoption: Strategies for AI governance and workforce engagement," discusses the barriers that organizations face to greater adoption of AI tools, one of them being the fact that 78% of organizations do not have a specific budget for AI investments. We provide recommendations for how to overcome those barriers by developing a comprehensive AI governance model and implementing employee-centered adoption interventions. 

The second report, "HR’s guide to improving AI literacy and AI readiness across the business," centers around the importance of improving AI literacy across the workforce in order to realize business outcomes from AI. In fact, our research shows that people with low AI literacy are between 4.3 and 11.8 times less likely to have used AI at work. This report also discusses the unique needs of employees, managers, and HR professionals when it comes to understanding AI and how to use it. 

To access other content from this research program, visit the Resources page of the HCM section on SAP.com. We hope you find it useful in maximizing the value of AI for HR.
